subject: Ceremony leanness — ceremonies are thin judgment wrappers over deterministic Basher scripts; ceremonies become first-class observable workflows; skill-set rationalization (104→~56) before migration
title: Lean Ceremonies (script-backed) + Skill-Set Rationalization
situation: A pre-build inventory (S47, 2 reviewers) covered all 104 skill files in templates/commands/. Findings: (1) the core ceremonies (wakeup/closeout/savepoint/...) are partly script-backed already (closeout.sh, wai-exit.sh, generate_wakeup_brief.py, verify_engine.py) but still carry heavy mechanical logic inline (git audit, skill sync, base/teaching adoption, delta detection, staging writes) that belongs in deterministic scripts; (2) the skill set has large redundancy — ~21 -slim/-reference variant clusters + 6 dead/redirect skills — that would migrate as cruft if not rationalized first. The user's directive: ceremonies must be tight and lean on Basher scripts as much as possible.
why: The Agent-vs-Script principle: agents are expensive/slow/non-deterministic; anything that runs the same way every time must be a script. Lean ceremonies = lower latency, testable mechanical steps, reusable across providers, and an agent that spends its judgment only where judgment is needed. Rationalizing the skill set before migration means MyWheel carries a clean canonical set (~56), not 104 with duplicate variants and dead redirects — and the deprecations travel with teachings (no silent retirement).
audience: Basher — owns the extracted ceremony scripts (.claude / tools) + distributes the rationalized skill set
ceremony skills (wakeup/closeout/savepoint/...) — become thin wrappers calling scripts + composing judgment strings only
Observability spine — ceremonies emit workflow_step events + bolts (first-class observable workflows)
migration engine + Hygiene — execute the deprecate/merge during migration; deprecations get teachings

leanness_principle.rule: A ceremony skill body contains ONLY judgment (what to decide, what to compose, when to branch). Every mechanical step (file write, git op, JSON/index update, file scan, schema check, version bump, archival, sync) is a deterministic Basher script the ceremony invokes. v3 proved the pattern: git moved out of closeout into wai-exit.sh via a staging buffer.
leanness_principle.test: if a step always runs the same way given the same inputs, it is a script — not skill prose. A ceremony that still does mechanical work inline fails this spec.
leanness_principle.staging_buffer_pattern: ceremonies capture intent/state in a JSON staging buffer (track-buffer, closeout-staging, partial buffers); Basher scripts + the Stop hook coordinate the actual commits/pushes asynchronously — keeps CC latency low + guarantees atomicity without nested tool calls.

script_extraction_targets.does: classify session into CONVERSATION_ONLY|MICRO|STANDARD|FULL — gates all subsequent ceremony steps
run base adoption, emit bolt, update version pointer
apply unadopted teachings, move to processed, create tracking lug
validate + route incoming lugs to bytype/
slug derive, file scan, JSON writes, pointer update, track append, brief regen (was a sub-agent — make it pure script)

script_extraction_targets.note: these are already Basher/tools-owned — extend, don't duplicate

ceremonies_as_workflows.principle: The KEEP ceremonies become first-class observable workflows (Stream N) — live per-step status + typed events + bolts.

workflows.emits: session_start event, base-adoption bolt (if needed), teaching-adoption tracking lug, wakeup brief
closeout event (terminal track), ceremony bolt (step results), disruption lug (if failures), fingerprint/staging
savepoint file (pending/active/completed), track event, pattern bolt (if pattern advanced)
track event, tracking lug (in_progress->completed), file move to processed/

ceremony_merges.merge: wai-slim -> wai (fast path already in wai)
wai-closeout-slim -> wai-closeout (delta-detect gives the level; slim = conditional branch)

deprecate_hard.reason: orphaned reference for deprecated skill (437 lines)
incomplete observation-logging stub, stale since Feb, never completed

skill_rationalization.merge_clusters: ~21 X.md + X-slim.md + X-reference.md trios/quartets -> single canonical X.md with progressive-disclosure sections (Fast Path callout + Reference appendix). Full cluster list in S47 inventory (track). Keep feedback-specific variants (e.g. wai-closeout-feedback-v2-slim) — not generic -slim cruft.
skill_rationalization.execution: EXECUTED DURING MIGRATION (Hygiene + Basher distribution), NOT now: deprecate files -> trash_bin (never rm) with a deprecation teaching each (no silent retirement); merges -> consolidate canonical then drop variants. MyWheel migrates the lean ~56 set.

constraints: A ceremony skill body is judgment-only; all mechanical work is a Basher script it invokes (Agent-vs-Script)
Extracted scripts are deterministic, idempotent, testable, and emit observability events (no silent script runs)
Ceremonies use the staging-buffer pattern; the Stop hook + Basher scripts own the actual commit/push
KEEP ceremonies are modeled as first-class observable workflows (live status + events + bolts)
Skill rationalization runs during migration, not ad hoc; every deprecation ships a teaching (no silent retirement); deprecated files go to trash_bin, never rm
Reference/schema-heavy skills (lug-schema, advisor-schema, crew-*) may relocate to managed/docs as reference, not executable skills
tests: Leanness audit: a KEEP ceremony's body has no inline mechanical step that a script could do (every git/file/json/index op is a script call)
Script determinism: each extracted script is idempotent (run twice = same result) + emits a typed event
Closeout delta: closeout-delta-detect.py classifies a conversation-only session as CONVERSATION_ONLY and the ceremony skips version bump/skill-sync/test-gate accordingly
Workflow observability: running closeout produces a closeout workflow object with live per-step status + a ceremony bolt
Rationalization: post-migration the skill set is ~56 canonical; the 6 deprecate files are in trash_bin with a deprecation teaching each; no -slim/-reference duplicate remains for a merged cluster
No silent retirement: each deprecated skill has a corresponding teaching in the hub teaching repo

open_questions: which reference-heavy skills relocate to managed/docs vs stay as skills (lug-schema/advisor-schema/crew-*)
wai-next: referenced in commands but file missing — create a thin 'what's next' or formally deprecate

perceive: Read this spec + the S47 ceremony inventory (track) + templates/commands/ ceremony bodies + existing scripts (closeout.sh, wai-exit.sh, generate_wakeup_brief.py). Confirm Basher ownership of .claude/* + ceremony scripts.
execute: 1. Extract the script_extraction_targets to Basher scripts (deterministic, idempotent, event-emitting). 2. Rewrite KEEP ceremonies as thin judgment wrappers invoking those scripts. 3. Model the KEEP ceremonies as observable workflow objects (Stream N). 4. During migration: collapse the 21 merge clusters into canonical files, deprecate the 6 dead files to trash_bin with teachings. 5. Regenerate the skill manifest from the lean set.
verify: Run all tests[]. Confirm ceremonies are judgment-only + script-backed, scripts are deterministic + observable, closeout delta gating works, ceremonies render as workflows, and the migrated skill set is the lean ~56 with deprecation teachings.
